title: Wolffiella repanda

intro: Wolffiella repanda is a species of aquatic plant, specifically a type of duckweed, belonging to the family Lemnaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, due to its antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Wolffiella repanda include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Wolffiella repanda is often used to prepare infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat fever, cough, and digestive problems.
